automotive-ev-battery.title: Honda to end Prologue sales in the US
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Honda has announced it will discontinue the Prologue after the 2026 model year, ending its partnership with General Motors on Ultium-based electric vehicles. This decision leaves Honda without a battery electric vehicle offering in the North American market, signaling a strategic shift in the automaker's EV strategy.

automotive-ev-battery.title: MAN starts electric truck production in Kraków
automotive-ev-battery.summary: MAN Truck & Bus is investing €1 billion in its Polish Kraków facility to expand capacity and serve as its second electric truck production site. Series production of the eTGL will commence in mid-July, with the new eTGM following later in 2026, marking a significant commitment to commercial vehicle electrification.

automotive-ev-battery.title: China introduces consumption tax on lithium-ion batteries
automotive-ev-battery.summary: China is ending a decade-long tax exemption for lithium-ion batteries effective September 2026, implementing a new consumption tax on traditional lithium-based batteries. The policy maintains exemptions for sodium-ion and solid-state batteries, strategically incentivizing development of next-generation battery technologies.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Mercedes confirms next-generation GLA for Rastatt plant
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Mercedes-Benz will launch the third-generation GLA as an electric-first compact SUV with battery-electric versions arriving later this year and hybrid variants in 2027. Production will remain at the Rastatt plant in Germany, a decision that confirms the facility's role in the automaker's EV transition despite ongoing cost-cutting measures.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Isuzu tests battery swapping for electric refuse trucks
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Isuzu is conducting pilot testing of swappable battery technology for electric refuse trucks in Yokohama, Japan, enabling battery exchanges within minutes. This approach aims to reduce vehicle downtime and increase operational availability for waste collection operations.

automotive-ev-battery.title: ChargePoint expands charging network in eastern US
automotive-ev-battery.summary: ChargePoint announced new charging infrastructure partnerships to deploy over 200 public charging ports across the Southeast and ultra-fast charging stations at 12 highway travel plazas in Pennsylvania and New York. The expansion targets major corridors to improve EV charging accessibility in the eastern United States.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Horse Powertrain presents methanol range extender
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Horse Powertrain unveiled a methanol-based range extender system combining a 2.0-liter turbocharged engine with a generator to improve EV efficiency. The powertrain incorporates an axial flux generator for the first time, offering a more compact and power-dense alternative to conventional designs.

automotive-ev-battery.title: How To Climb a Critical Mineral Mountain? Come Together, Right Now
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Industry, academic, and government stakeholders convened at the 2026 Partner Forum to address U.S. critical mineral supply chain challenges essential for EV battery production. The discussion focused on domestic mining capabilities and strategies to reduce supply chain vulnerabilities in advanced materials.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Hungary: Former foreign minister joins BYD
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Former Hungarian Foreign Minister Péter Szijjártó has joined BYD in a private sector role, a move reflecting his instrumental involvement in bringing the Chinese automaker to Hungary. The appointment is expected to prove controversial given his public role in facilitating BYD's market entry.

automotive-ev-battery.title: New Charging Station Plan Offers More Proof-Of-Life For EVs
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Charging infrastructure startup Rangeway is introducing an EV-focused charging facility model inspired by hospitality industry design principles. The initiative demonstrates continued private sector investment in EV charging infrastructure and consumer experience enhancement.

tech-ai.title: How Apple's big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI's IPO plans
tech-ai.summary: Apple has filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI alleging misconduct including the recruitment of over 400 former Apple employees and claims reaching to OpenAI's chief hardware officer. The lawsuit's timing is particularly significant as OpenAI reportedly prepares for an initial public offering, with the company's response thus far remaining carefully measured.

tech-ai.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI's favorite second act
tech-ai.summary: Databricks has achieved a $188 billion valuation while successfully repositioning itself as an artificial intelligence company, reinforcing its status as a leading player in the AI infrastructure sector. The company has published research demonstrating cost savings benefits of open-weight AI models for coding applications.

tech-ai.title: Samsung's redesigned Z Fold 8 with a wide display just leaked
tech-ai.summary: Leaked images reveal Samsung's upcoming Galaxy Z Fold 8 foldable smartphone with a shorter, wider design and specifications including the Snapdragon 8 Elite For Galaxy processor and dual 50MP rear cameras. The device is expected to be officially announced at Samsung's July 22nd launch event.

tech-ai.title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
tech-ai.summary: TikTok is piloting an opt-in tool that enables creators to identify and report artificially generated likenesses of themselves on the platform, initially available to select U.S. creators for identity verification testing. The initiative mirrors similar efforts by YouTube, which recently expanded its AI detection capabilities to all adult users.

tech-ai.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India's smartphone market
tech-ai.summary: India's smartphone market is experiencing a slowdown driven by the artificial intelligence boom, which is reshaping consumer electronics through pricing pressures, shifting demand patterns, and evolving corporate strategies. The trend underscores how AI adoption is fundamentally altering the consumer electronics landscape in key markets.

tech-ai.title: Nuclear startup Valar Atomics in talks to raise new funding at $6B valuation
tech-ai.summary: Valar Atomics is pursuing a new funding round at a $6 billion valuation, exemplifying an emerging trend of complex, multi-stage funding mechanisms that can obscure actual entry valuations. The deal reflects growing investor interest in advanced nuclear technology as an energy solution.

tech-ai.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here's how it actually performs
tech-ai.summary: Vertu's luxury foldable smartphone features an AI agent component at a premium price point, with the device offering distinctive features including AI workflows, battery performance, and security capabilities. The review assesses the real-world experience and value proposition of this high-end AI-enabled device.

tech-ai.title: The Zoom hack that says, 'Don't record me'
tech-ai.summary: The article examines the implications of ubiquitous meeting transcription and summarization technology, questioning the practical value when every conversation is automatically captured and processed. It raises concerns about information overload in a world where watercooler conversations, dates, and meetings are routinely transcribed.

tech-ai.title: Apple and Google ordered to purge 'nudify' apps from App Stores
tech-ai.summary: San Francisco City Attorney David Chiu has issued formal letters to Apple and Google demanding removal of non-consensual intimate image manipulation apps from their platforms, citing long-standing violations of state law. Both companies have allegedly been aware of these illegal applications while continuing to host them.

tech-ai.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla's backyard
tech-ai.summary: Agility Robotics is opening a new training facility for its Digit robots in Fremont, California, establishing a presence in Tesla's manufacturing hub and signaling expansion in advanced robotics commercialization. The move represents an effort to scale production and deployment of its humanoid robot technology.

world-news.title: Iran war live: US and Iran exchange attacks for 7th consecutive night
world-news.summary: The United States and Iran have engaged in escalating military exchanges for seven consecutive nights, with US strikes penetrating deeper into Iranian territory. The US military characterizes the operations as degrading Iranian military capabilities, while Iran alleges targeting of civilian infrastructure, raising regional stability concerns.

world-news.title: US denies Iranian claims it hit civilian infrastructure in latest strikes
world-news.summary: The US military maintains that recent attacks on Iran were designed to degrade Iranian military capabilities, contradicting Tehran's assertions that civilian infrastructure was targeted. The ongoing military confrontation between the two nations continues to escalate regional tensions.

world-news.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
world-news.summary: US President Trump has threatened additional tariffs against Canada amid cross-border air pollution from wildfires, while Canadian leader Mark Carney emphasizes shared climate responsibility. The dispute highlights tensions over environmental management and trade policy between the two nations.

world-news.title: EU urges Israel to halt settlement expansion as settlers attack children
world-news.summary: The European Union is intensifying pressure on Israel to cease illegal settlement expansion and address violence against Palestinians by settlers. Growing calls within the EU for sanctions against Israel reflect deepening international concern over the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.

world-news.title: Iraq signs deals with Western oil firms, including to revive Syria pipeline
world-news.summary: Iraq has signed agreements with Western oil companies, including Chevron, to reconstruct a crude oil pipeline extending from Iraq's Kirkuk region to Syria's Baniyas port. The deal represents significant geopolitical realignment and energy infrastructure development in the Middle East.

world-news.title: Many Ukrainian soldiers outraged over removal of defence minister, troops tell BBC
world-news.summary: Ukrainian soldiers have expressed significant outrage following the removal of Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, with widespread protests erupting across the military. The internal military discord raises questions about leadership stability amid ongoing conflict with Russia.

world-news.title: China hits out at British Steel nationalisation
world-news.summary: China has criticized the UK government's decision to nationalize British Steel, which London justified as protecting vital national industrial capability. The dispute reflects broader geopolitical tensions between Beijing and Western nations over strategic industrial assets.

world-news.title: EU proposes slowing down cuts to carbon emissions for businesses
world-news.summary: The European Union has proposed relaxing its emissions trading system to grant companies extended timelines for reducing carbon output. The policy adjustment signals a potential shift in Europe's climate strategy amid economic considerations.

world-news.title: One anti-war critic fined, another held as Russia clamps down on opponents
world-news.summary: Russia has intensified suppression of domestic dissent by fining anti-war critic Boris Nadezhdin and barring him from parliamentary candidacy, while blogger Ilya Remeslo has been remanded in custody. The crackdown reflects Moscow's broader campaign against political opposition and free expression.

world-news.title: Second tanker in three months hijacked off Yemen by suspected Somali pirates
world-news.summary: A second commercial tanker has been hijacked off the coast of Yemen in three months by suspected Somali pirates, indicating a resurgence of piracy in the region. The incidents signal renewed maritime security threats along a critical international shipping corridor.

science-space.title: Earth's largest particle accelerator opens new window into the early universe just after the Big Bang
science-space.summary: Earth's largest particle accelerator has achieved a significant breakthrough, providing new insights into the evolution of the early universe immediately following the Big Bang. This observation represents a culmination of decades-long research efforts to understand the fundamental conditions of the cosmos.

science-space.title: NASA Invites Media to Roman Space Telescope, Crew-13 Launches
science-space.summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for the launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, scheduled for no earlier than August 30, 2026, aboard a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket. The agency is also accepting credentials for the simultaneous SpaceX Crew-13 mission, marking two major launches in the coming months.

science-space.title: Making history: Watch Vikram-1, India's 1st private orbital rocket, launch early on July 18
science-space.summary: India's first privately-built orbital rocket, Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1, is scheduled to launch early Saturday morning on July 18, 2026, representing a historic milestone for India's commercial space industry. The launch will be available for public viewing.

science-space.title: The Perseid meteor shower is here! How to see the best shooting stars this summer
science-space.summary: The 2026 Perseid meteor shower is active and peaks alongside a dramatic total solar eclipse on August 12-13. Observers will have multiple opportunities throughout July and August to witness this popular annual celestial event.

science-space.title: NASA's Chandra and IXPE Study Pulsar in Lighthouse Nebula
science-space.summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory and the Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer have captured a detailed composite image of a rapidly spinning pulsar within the Lighthouse nebula. The multi-instrument observation reveals new details about this neutron star's powerful magnetic field and X-ray emissions.

science-space.title: NASA's Psyche Mission Images Details of Martian Surface During Flyby
science-space.summary: During its May 2026 flyby of Mars, NASA's Psyche spacecraft captured enhanced-color images of the Martian surface using its multispectral imager instrument. The high-resolution imagery provides detailed views of the Red Planet's geological features as the spacecraft conducted a gravity-assist maneuver.

science-space.title: NASA Pushes New Wing Design to Find Structural Limits
science-space.summary: NASA researchers subjected a new lightweight, long and thin wing design called SWEET-15 (Structural Wing Experiment Evaluating Truss-bracing) to rigorous structural testing to determine its limits. Results from the testing demonstrated encouraging potential for the wing design, even when pushed beyond its intended operational parameters.

science-space.title: These 'metallic' dunes on Mars look like sci-fi. What are they really?
science-space.summary: The European Space Agency's Mars Express orbiter has captured images of unusual wave-like formations on Mars that resemble metallic dunes. Scientists are investigating the composition and formation mechanisms of these distinctive Martian geological features.

science-space.title: Establishing a VTE Risk Score for Astronauts Algorithm
science-space.summary: In April 2026, NASA's Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer initiated a working group to review venous thromboembolism (VTE) case data and altered blood flow information among astronauts. The effort aims to develop improved risk mitigation strategies for spaceflight-related cardiovascular complications.

science-space.title: NASA Welcomes Mauritius as 70th Artemis Accords Signatory
science-space.summary: The Republic of Mauritius has officially joined the Artemis Accords, becoming the 70th signatory nation and the seventh African country to commit to responsible space exploration principles. The signing ceremony highlighted NASA's ongoing efforts to build a global coalition for sustainable and peaceful space activities.

finance-markets.title: Chip Stocks Slide Into Bear Market in AI Unwind: Markets Wrap
finance-markets.summary: Semiconductor stocks have entered bear market territory as investors reassess the sustainability of artificial intelligence spending amid concerns about elevated valuations. The selloff reflects growing skepticism about whether current AI investment levels can be justified by future profitability.

finance-markets.title: AI Upheaval Crushes Retail Traders Crowding Into Leveraged Funds
finance-markets.summary: A breakthrough artificial intelligence model from Chinese startup Moonshot triggered a sharp selloff in AI and semiconductor stocks, causing significant losses for retail investors holding leveraged positions in these sectors. The market disruption echoes the 'DeepSeek moment' and underscores rapid shifts in technology sector valuations.

finance-markets.title: US chip stocks notch up worst week in more than a year
finance-markets.summary: Semiconductor shares experienced their worst weekly performance in over a year as momentum-driven trades reversed amid renewed concerns about artificial intelligence valuations. The selloff has affected major chipmakers and raised questions about the sustainability of the AI investment boom.

finance-markets.title: US Stocks End Week Lower on Fears of Another 'DeepSeek Moment'
finance-markets.summary: U.S. stock markets declined over the week as semiconductor companies fell following the announcement of a competitive artificial intelligence model from a Chinese startup. Investor concerns about AI valuations and competitive threats drove broader market weakness.

finance-markets.title: Apple briefly leapfrogs Nvidia as world's most valuable company
finance-markets.summary: Apple temporarily surpassed Nvidia as the world's most valuable company as investors rotated away from chipmakers amid AI selloff concerns. The shift reflects diverging fortunes in the technology sector as semiconductor stocks face renewed valuation pressures.

finance-markets.title: 'Sea of Red' Hits Emerging Stocks as AI Concerns Jolts Markets
finance-markets.summary: Emerging-market stocks experienced significant losses as a key gauge slipped into technical correction territory amid mounting concerns that artificial intelligence industry valuations are no longer justified. The sell-off reflects global investor reassessment of technology sector fundamentals.

finance-markets.title: Meta and Anthropic in talks for up to $10bn data centre deal
finance-markets.summary: Meta is considering launching a cloud business and may commit up to $10 billion to a data centre partnership with Anthropic as part of its $145 billion infrastructure spending programme. The deal reflects major technology companies' aggressive investment in artificial intelligence infrastructure and capacity.

finance-markets.title: Gauge of Oracle's Credit Risk Hits Fresh All Time High
finance-markets.summary: A measure of Oracle's credit risk reached record highs as investors worry about the company's cash burn rate and the future profitability of its artificial intelligence investments amid new competitive threats from Chinese AI models. The spike reflects broader concerns about technology companies' ability to monetize AI investments.

finance-markets.title: How long can oil markets absorb the Hormuz shock?
finance-markets.summary: While improvements in energy efficiency have cushioned the global economy against oil supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, concerns persist that geopolitical tensions could prompt risky policy responses. The analysis examines the limits of structural adjustments in protecting oil markets from regional conflicts.

finance-markets.title: CoreLogic Wraps Up $5.25 Billion Debt Deal With Hefty Yields
finance-markets.summary: CoreLogic successfully completed a $5.25 billion debt offering after adjusting terms and offering elevated yields to attract investors amid lukewarm market demand. The transaction underscores current credit market challenges and the need for issuers to provide higher returns to secure funding.

health-biotech.title: GSK drops Phase 3 chronic cough drug in a blow to $2B Bellus Health buyout
health-biotech.summary: GlaxoSmithKline has terminated development of camlipixant for refractory chronic cough, marking a significant setback for its $2 billion acquisition of Bellus Health. The Phase 3 discontinuation represents a major clinical and financial disappointment for the company's pipeline.

health-biotech.title: GSK plans large study to see if shingles vaccine can prevent dementia
health-biotech.summary: GlaxoSmithKline is launching a major clinical study investigating whether shingles vaccination can reduce Alzheimer's disease risk, following epidemiological data suggesting vaccinated individuals have lower dementia incidence. The study represents a novel therapeutic application for an established vaccine.

health-biotech.title: Celcuity got its cancer drug approved. Then its stock dipped
health-biotech.summary: The FDA approved Celcuity's cancer drug Revtorpyk, but investor enthusiasm dampened due to delayed commercial launch timing extending into the end of the quarter. The approval-to-market timeline mismatch created unexpected market volatility for the company.

health-biotech.title: Lettuce at Taco Bell in five states confirmed as a source of diarrhea-causing parasite
health-biotech.summary: Health authorities have identified Mexican lettuce served at Taco Bell locations across five U.S. states as the source of a cyclospora outbreak causing diarrheal illness. The investigation highlights ongoing food safety challenges in the supply chain for fresh produce.

health-biotech.title: Quitting smoking is hard. A Medicare change may push doctors to give more help
health-biotech.summary: Medicare has proposed a 19% reimbursement increase for physicians who provide counseling on smoking and alcohol cessation during patient visits. The policy change aims to incentivize preventive intervention and improve quit rates among Medicare beneficiaries.

health-biotech.title: Pharmalittle: We're reading about rising drug shortages, GSK coughing up a trial loss, and more
health-biotech.summary: The number of active medication shortages in the U.S. has risen to 227 in the second quarter of 2026, reflecting ongoing supply chain and manufacturing challenges across the pharmaceutical industry. The report also covers GSK's recent clinical trial setbacks and other sector developments.

health-biotech.title: Midsize biotechs press White House to drop CMS pricing rules
health-biotech.summary: Several midsize biotech companies have met with White House and HHS officials to oppose two pending CMS pricing models, arguing the rules could impair business operations and innovation. The lobbying effort reflects industry concerns about regulatory impact on smaller pharmaceutical firms.

health-biotech.title: Jasper gets a lifeline in merger with immune drugmaker Kira
health-biotech.summary: Jasper Therapeutics has merged with immune-focused drugmaker Kira in a transaction that includes a concurrent licensing agreement with startup Mirador, providing the company recovery from significant market value losses. The strategic combination aims to strengthen Jasper's pipeline and commercial viability.

health-biotech.title: Dimerix buys a kidney injury drug; Arpeggio appears to be winding down
health-biotech.summary: Dimerix has acquired a Phase 2-ready acute kidney injury candidate from Mission Therapeutics for $5 million upfront, while Arpeggio Therapeutics appears to be winding down operations. The transaction reflects continued M&A activity and consolidation in the biotech sector.

health-biotech.title: Why affirming trans identities can be critical for suicide prevention counseling
health-biotech.summary: Advocates express concerns that excluding transgender youth from LGBTQ+ counseling services provided through the 988 crisis line could prove harmful and counterproductive to suicide prevention efforts. The debate highlights the clinical importance of affirming care in mental health crisis interventions.

crypto-web3.title: Japan's SBI Group is building Asia's first cross-border digital asset empire
crypto-web3.summary: SBI Holdings has consolidated Singapore-based Coinhako as part of a major regional expansion strategy in digital assets. The securities giant is also partnering with Ondo Finance on tokenization initiatives, positioning itself as a dominant player in Asia's emerging crypto infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Stripe and Swift race to control the next generation of global payments infrastructure
crypto-web3.summary: Traditional finance giants Stripe and Swift are increasingly competing to dominate the infrastructure behind digital payments, according to crypto and blockchain experts. This week's strategic moves signal an escalating battle for control of the evolving payments ecosystem.

crypto-web3.title: Bank of America taps new leaders to bridge crypto, AI and traditional finance
crypto-web3.summary: Bank of America appointed Sonali Theisen as head of its global digital assets platform and Kevin Milsom as head of AI transformation. The moves signal the institution's commitment to integrating cryptocurrency and artificial intelligence into its core business operations.

crypto-web3.title: Inside Robinhood's high-stakes bet to onboard millions of casual users onto decentralized finance
crypto-web3.summary: Robinhood is leveraging its retail customer base to drive mainstream adoption of decentralized finance, though early activity has been dominated by memecoins rather than its original tokenization vision. The company's strategy remains a work in progress as it attempts to bridge traditional retail investors with on-chain opportunities.

crypto-web3.title: Cardano hands core development to outside teams in decentralization push
crypto-web3.summary: Input Output announced plans to transfer control of core Cardano components including the Haskell node, Plutus, and Hydra to external development teams. Founder Charles Hoskinson stated that the network must decentralize and resume growth under this new governance structure.

crypto-web3.title: FTX to distribute roughly $900 million to creditors in fifth wave of payouts
crypto-web3.summary: FTX's bankruptcy estate will distribute approximately $900 million to creditors in its fifth wave of repayments since distributions began in 2025. The company has distributed nearly $10 billion to creditors and claimants to date, representing significant progress in the bankruptcy resolution process.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto-style derivatives reach AI compute ahead of planned CME and ICE futures
crypto-web3.summary: Crypto-style perpetual contracts and prediction markets have emerged in the AI compute sector ahead of regulated GPU futures expected in late 2026. The development signals growing institutional interest in derivatives markets for alternative assets, according to Bernstein research.

crypto-web3.title: Polymarket traders cut Clarity Act passage odds to record low as Senate delay drags on
crypto-web3.summary: Prediction market traders on Polymarket have reduced the odds of the CLARITY Act passing this year to historic lows as Senate negotiations over ethics provisions remain unresolved. The declining odds reflect market skepticism about the legislation's prospects amid ongoing legislative delays.

crypto-web3.title: Taiwan sentences BitShine crypto exchange ringleader to 22 years in $39 million fraud case
crypto-web3.summary: A Taiwan court sentenced the operator of BitShine, a cryptocurrency exchange formerly registered with the financial regulator, to 22 years imprisonment for operating a fraudulent scheme that defrauded victims of approximately $39 million. The conviction highlights ongoing regulatory enforcement against illicit crypto exchange operations.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin ETFs could mirror gold's history of 'spectacular gains' and 'painful drawdowns,' analyst says
crypto-web3.summary: Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas compared Bitcoin's IBIT ETF trajectory to gold's GLD fund, which experienced rapid growth followed by significant volatility after its 2011 inception. The analysis suggests Bitcoin ETFs may follow a similar pattern of dramatic appreciation interspersed with substantial corrections.

startups-vc.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI's favorite second act
startups-vc.summary: Databricks has achieved a $188 billion valuation, cementing its position as one of the most valuable AI-focused companies and demonstrating strong investor confidence in established platforms pivoting to AI infrastructure. The company has bolstered its market position through published research highlighting cost savings of open-weight AI models for coding applications.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: No Summer Doldrums As Dollars Still Flow To AI
startups-vc.summary: A robust week of venture funding saw multiple major rounds close across AI and enterprise sectors, with enterprise AI startup Fireworks AI leading at $1.5 billion and meal delivery provider Wonder completing a Series D. The continued capital flow demonstrates sustained investor appetite despite seasonal patterns.

startups-vc.title: Nuclear startup Valar Atomics in talks to raise new funding at $6B valuation
startups-vc.summary: Nuclear energy startup Valar Atomics is negotiating a funding round at a $6 billion valuation, highlighting the emerging trend of complex, multi-stage financing structures that can obscure true market entry prices. The deal reflects growing investor interest in nuclear technology as a climate solution.

startups-vc.title: Sheryl Sandberg leads $10 million investment in AI-powered vehicle inspection service
startups-vc.summary: Former Meta COO Sheryl Sandberg led a $10 million investment in an AI-powered vehicle inspection startup founded in 2021, which enables enterprise customers to use smartphones to identify vehicle damage. The backing from a prominent tech executive signals growing mainstream adoption of AI in insurance and fleet management sectors.

startups-vc.title: 20VC x SaaStr This Week: Apple Sues OpenAI, the Token-Maxing Era Begins, and the TAM Question Hanging Over AI Coding
startups-vc.summary: Apple filed a significant trade secret theft lawsuit against OpenAI involving alleged theft of physical prototypes by multiple Apple employees now at OpenAI, raising serious corporate governance and talent mobility concerns within the AI industry. The lawsuit underscores escalating tensions between major tech players competing in the AI space.

startups-vc.title: No product? No problem. This Disrupt 2026 session shows how to get pre-seed funding with conviction, storytelling
startups-vc.summary: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 will feature a session addressing the challenges pre-seed founders face as AI startups absorb disproportionate seed funding and raise bars across the ecosystem. The session aims to provide guidance on securing pre-seed capital through compelling storytelling and founder conviction despite increased competition.

startups-vc.title: Christopher Golda and Grey Baker Join YC as General Partners
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator announced the appointment of Christopher Golda and Grey Baker as General Partners, strengthening the organization's investment leadership team. The additions reflect YC's expansion of its partnership capacity during a period of continued innovation in emerging technologies.

startups-vc.title: Ultrahuman's former hardware VP raises $5.5M for devices that control AI agents, not just record you
startups-vc.summary: A former Ultrahuman hardware vice president raised $5.5 million for Aina, a startup developing devices designed to control AI agents rather than merely recording user data. The company plans to pilot its new device in the coming weeks, signaling a shift toward user-centric AI hardware design.

startups-vc.title: So Far, 2026 Is A Solid Year For Cybersecurity Startup Funding
startups-vc.summary: Cybersecurity and privacy startups raised $4.4 billion in Q2 2026 across seed through growth-stage rounds, representing a 30% decline from Q1 but maintaining robust funding levels relative to historical trends. The quarter demonstrated sustained investor confidence in the security sector despite typical seasonal variations.

startups-vc.title: 5 Interesting Learnings from Zoom at ~$5 Billion in ARR: Modestly Re-Accelerating Growth, Real AI Monetization, a $27B Market Cap, and a 25x Anthropic Windfall
startups-vc.summary: Zoom has recovered from years of decline following the post-pandemic normalization to reach $5 billion in ARR with modest re-acceleration driven by real AI monetization initiatives. The company's trajectory demonstrates how established platforms can leverage AI capabilities to reinvigorate growth and unlock significant shareholder value.

cybersecurity.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft released software updates addressing at least 570 security vulnerabilities across Windows and other products, nearly triple the previous month's record-breaking patch count. The software giant attributed the significant increase in discovered flaws to advances in artificial intelligence-aided vulnerability detection.

cybersecurity.title: New wp2shell WordPress Core Flaw Lets Unauthenticated Attackers Run Code
cybersecurity.summary: A critical vulnerability in WordPress core versions 6.9 and 7.0 allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code through anonymous HTTP requests, affecting even bare installations with no plugins. WordPress released patches on Friday and enabled forced automatic updates to mitigate the widespread threat.

cybersecurity.title: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet
cybersecurity.summary: The FBI announced the seizure of hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a residential proxy service operated by Israeli firm Alarum Technologies, following evidence linking it to the Popa botnet comprising at least two million compromised devices. The law enforcement action follows security research published by KrebsOnSecurity connecting the platform to malicious activities.

cybersecurity.title: Seven Malicious Vite npm Packages Use Blockchain C2 to Deliver a RAT
cybersecurity.summary: Researchers identified a supply chain attack involving seven malicious npm packages targeting the Vite frontend framework, utilizing an advanced blockchain-based command-and-control infrastructure spanning multiple blockchain networks. The campaign, dubbed ViteVenom, represents an expansion of the ChainVeil threat operation targeting software developers.

cybersecurity.title: New NadMesh Botnet Hunts Exposed AI Services for Cloud Keys and Kubernetes Tokens
cybersecurity.summary: A newly discovered Go-based botnet named NadMesh actively hunts for exposed artificial intelligence services to steal cloud credentials and Kubernetes tokens, with the attacker's dashboard claiming over 3,800 AWS keys harvested. The botnet targets popular AI tools including ComfyUI, Ollama, and Gradio deployed with inadequate network security controls.

cybersecurity.title: Inc Ransomware Exploits SonicWall SMA Zero-Days
cybersecurity.summary: Inc ransomware group exploited two previously unknown vulnerabilities in SonicWall's Secure Mobile Access appliances that, when chained together, grant attackers root-level system access. This zero-day exploitation demonstrates the active threat to widely deployed enterprise remote access infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: GoldenEyeDog Subgroup Linked to DigiCert Breach and Code-Signing Certificate Theft
cybersecurity.summary: Security researchers attributed the April 2026 DigiCert incident to a Chinese threat group called CylindricalCanine, identified as a sub-group of the GoldenEyeDog cybercriminal collective known for targeting gaming and gambling sectors. The breach resulted in the theft of code-signing certificates, enabling attackers to sign malicious software with legitimate credentials.

cybersecurity.title: ACR Stealer Uses ClickFix Lures to Steal Browser Tokens and Microsoft 365 Files
cybersecurity.summary: ACR Stealer, an active infostealer since 2024, infiltrates enterprise networks through social engineering tactics and exfiltrates saved browser credentials, session tokens, Microsoft 365 documents, and OneDrive files. Microsoft's Defender Experts team identified multiple delivery chains used to distribute the malware within corporate environments.

cybersecurity.title: Fake Coding Tests Deliver OtterCookie-Aligned Malware Hidden in SVG Flag Images
cybersecurity.summary: North Korean threat actors affiliated with the Contagious Interview campaign are exploiting fake job postings and coding challenges to distribute malware, using steganography to conceal payloads within SVG image files. The four-stage attack delivers OtterCookie, a sophisticated malware capable of stealing browser credentials, cryptocurrency wallets, and files.

cybersecurity.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
cybersecurity.summary: A denial-of-service vulnerability in OpenSSL, named HollowByte by Okta's Red Team, allows attackers to exhaust server memory by sending minimal 11-byte TLS requests that cause systems to reserve up to 131 KB per connection. OpenSSL patched the flaw in June without public disclosure, highlighting potential security gaps in critical infrastructure libraries.

energy-climate.title: Debriefed 17 July 2026: UK 'firewave' | Fossil-fuelled heat deaths | London's Natural History Museum spotlights climate
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's weekly digest covers the UK's deadly heat emergency and its connection to fossil fuel use, alongside major climate awareness initiatives. The report documents the escalating impacts of climate change on public health and safety across the region.

energy-climate.title: Q&A: Europe's May and June heatwave deaths – and how they were counted
energy-climate.summary: This analysis examines the methodologies used by public health authorities and scientists to quantify mortality from Europe's recent severe heatwaves. The article provides critical context on how heat-related deaths are measured and attributed to climate impacts.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 10 July 2026: Deadly Europe heat | EU electrification leak | COP31 president interview
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's weekly update covers Europe's deadly heat emergency, leaked EU electrification strategy documents, and interviews with COP31 leadership on climate policy direction. The report highlights both the immediate climate impacts and international policy responses.

energy-climate.title: 28 quotes from next UK leader Andy Burnham on climate, net-zero and fossil fuels
energy-climate.summary: This compilation examines the climate and energy positions of incoming UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham through direct quotations on net-zero policy and fossil fuel transition. The article provides critical insight into the incoming government's climate agenda.

energy-climate.title: OMV secures $512m EIB loan for green hydrogen plant in Austria
energy-climate.summary: OMV has obtained a €450 million ($512 million) European Investment Bank loan to support construction of its flagship green hydrogen production facility in Austria. The investment represents significant capital mobilization for hydrogen infrastructure development in Europe.

energy-climate.title: China imposes new consumption tax on PV cells, batteries amid industry consolidation push
energy-climate.summary: China has introduced a new consumption tax on photovoltaic cells and batteries alongside stricter energy efficiency standards as part of efforts to reduce industry overcapacity. The policy aims to consolidate the solar sector and favor higher-efficiency producers across the value chain.

energy-climate.title: India switches on first hydrogen train
energy-climate.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has inaugurated India's first hydrogen fuel cell-powered train, marking a major milestone in the nation's sustainable transportation transition. The train will initially operate on the 89-kilometer Jind–Sonipat route in Haryana state.

energy-climate.title: Chinese PV Industry Brief: module manufacturers forecast heavy losses as oversupply persists
energy-climate.summary: Major Chinese solar module manufacturers including Tongwei, JA, Longi, and Trina Solar have forecast significant losses for the first half of 2026 due to persistent oversupply and weak demand. The announcements underscore continued structural challenges throughout the global solar supply chain.

energy-climate.title: China's battery boom pivoting from scale to utilization, says Ember
energy-climate.summary: China's battery storage sector is transitioning from mandate-driven capacity expansion toward market-driven optimization as the country targets 300 GW of new energy storage by 2030. The shift reflects maturation of the Chinese battery industry toward efficiency and utilization metrics.

energy-climate.title: IDF and Oaktree back $1.7bn Bloom Energy project for AI infrastructure
energy-climate.summary: The Israel Innovation Fund and Oaktree Capital have announced a $1.7 billion investment to deploy Bloom Energy's fuel cell technology, addressing power demands from artificial intelligence infrastructure. The investment reflects growing capital commitment to fuel cell technology for large-scale industrial applications.

asia-pacific.title: China's missile test from a submarine is no guarantee of regional stability
asia-pacific.summary: China successfully tested an intercontinental-range submarine-launched ballistic missile on July 6, joining an elite club of nuclear powers with proven SSBN capabilities. Analysts believe the test, conducted from a Type 094 Jin-class nuclear submarine, represents a significant advancement in China's strategic deterrent capabilities, though experts caution it does not guarantee regional stability.

asia-pacific.title: China's Xpeng plants a flag in Germany's auto heartland with Mona L03 launch
asia-pacific.summary: Chinese electric vehicle manufacturer Xpeng unveiled its Mona L03 compact SUV in Munich, marking the largest European launch by a Chinese automaker to date and signaling serious long-term commitment to the European market. The launch demonstrates China's strategic push to establish manufacturing and sales presence in Europe's automotive sector amid intensifying trade competition.

asia-pacific.title: China, North Korea Exchange High-Level Visits
asia-pacific.summary: China and North Korea are conducting a series of high-level diplomatic exchanges as Beijing seeks to maintain its strategic relationship with Pyongyang amid the latter's deepening military and economic ties with Russia. These visits underscore China's efforts to counter Moscow's growing influence in the Korean Peninsula.

asia-pacific.title: What India's Operationalization of Integrated Battle Groups Means for Pakistan
asia-pacific.summary: India has operationalized its Integrated Battle Groups, military formations originally designed for offensive operations against Pakistan under conditions of nuclear deterrence. The development represents a significant shift in India's military doctrine and strategic posture in South Asia.

asia-pacific.title: Is Taiwan Pinning Its Strategy on an Outdated Vision of America?
asia-pacific.summary: Taiwan's strategic calculations regarding U.S. security commitments may not account for shifting generational attitudes and Trump administration transactionalism in foreign policy. The article suggests Taiwan should adopt a more diversified approach to regional security, drawing lessons from Singapore's strategic autonomy.

asia-pacific.title: Asia's AI boom has a dirty secret: the coal that powers it
asia-pacific.summary: Asia's rapid expansion of artificial intelligence infrastructure is heavily dependent on coal power, with the region accounting for nearly three-fifths of global coal reserves. The energy demand from AI deployment is outpacing the commissioning of clean energy infrastructure, raising environmental and sustainability concerns across the region.

asia-pacific.title: Papua New Guinea Just Closed Taiwan's Economic Office
asia-pacific.summary: Papua New Guinea's closure of Taiwan's economic office marks a significant reduction in Taiwan's diplomatic presence in the strategically important Pacific region along a critical energy corridor. The move reflects Taiwan's shrinking diplomatic space amid Beijing's continued efforts to isolate the island internationally.

asia-pacific.title: EU-Japan-South Korea Cooperation Is Indispensable Now
asia-pacific.summary: Trilateral cooperation between the European Union, Japan, and South Korea has become increasingly important and feasible as these democracies seek to counter authoritarian influence and establish alternative economic and security frameworks. The partnership addresses shared concerns about regional stability and technology competition.

asia-pacific.title: Pakistan Adopts Wait-and-Watch Approach as US and Iran Return to Hostilities
asia-pacific.summary: Pakistan is maintaining a cautious stance amid renewed tensions between the United States and Iran, continuing to prioritize the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding as the framework for de-escalation in regional conflicts. This reflects Pakistan's efforts to maintain strategic balance between competing regional powers.

asia-pacific.title: Progress on the China-Kyrgyzstan-Uzbekistan Railway, Some Challenges Remain
asia-pacific.summary: The China-Kyrgyzstan-Uzbekistan Railway project, under discussion for two decades, has begun construction with significant hurdles already addressed, though technical and logistical challenges persist. The rail link represents an important component of China's Belt and Road Initiative in Central Asia and regional connectivity infrastructure.
